if you have money that's been damaged in

we'll evaluate and determine how much is

my name is eric walsh i'm the assistant
manager of the immunity currency
division
every year the immunity currency
division receives about 23 000 cases
people will send money that's been
somehow damaged and re-reimbursed
annually about 40 million dollars as a
free service the government provides
that we pay a dollar in dollar out
we require 51 of the note present to pay
on it and that just protects us from
paying twice on the same note
once we determine the amount we will
send a treasury check for what we
discover
we're pretty low tech here so the tools
of the trade are scissors knives
scalpels glue and tape and maybe a pair
of tweezers
the most challenging cases are usually
cases that the money has gotten wet and
set for long periods of times and it
will actually petrify and be solid as a
brick
a lot of is the dog ate my money we get
hundreds of those cases a year a lot of
people will hide their money in the oven
and forget about it or they'll get it
wet and put in the microwave and that
will ignite the currency
our most infamous case took place in the
80s and what happened was a farmer lost
his wallet in the field and discovered
that his cow had eaten it and we always
encouraged the submitter to send in the
package as it is and the farmer sent in
the cow's stomach which we were able to
retrieve the wallet from and reimburse
the farmer his currency
[Music]
our office consists of just under 20
people and we have 12 people that
examine and process the submissions
and they do roughly between 1 000 2
thousand claims each year
we do get an increased amount of cases
after a national disaster
over the past year between the
hurricanes and wildfires out in
california we've reimbursed over two
million dollars we're called upon a lot
of people hit their low point so any
relief that you could provide someone
after they lost all their money in a

it's very rewarding
hopefully we're a service that you'll
